findings.title: Public arbitrary-token approval lets any caller steal the market's USDT, cUSDT, and other ERC-20 balances

best_agent_match.title: Public arbitrary-token approval lets any caller drain the market's USDT and cToken balances

findings.title: Redeem burns full DepTokens even when Compound withdrawal fails and only partial cash is available

best_agent_match.title: Redeem burns full DepTokens even when external liquidity retrieval fails and only a partial payout is available

findings.title: Curve swaps use zero minimum output, enabling sandwich extraction on borrow and refund flows

best_agent_match.title: All Curve swaps execute with zero slippage protection, enabling sandwich extraction and value loss

findings.title: Ignored Compound mint errors can brick later USDT resupply attempts via stale allowance

best_agent_match.title: Ignored Compound mint errors can leave a stuck non-zero allowance and permanently DoS future supply attempts

rejected_candidates.title: Missing nonReentrant guard on updateBorrowLedger allows reentrancy attack
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. `updateBorrowLedger` is internal-only, and every reachable caller in this codebase (`redeemInternal` and `repayBorrowInternal`) already runs under the contract-level `nonReentrant` guard.

rejected_candidates.title: Admin can set malicious Compound and Curve addresses to steal user funds
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. This is an admin-trust/governance-risk observation, not a standalone code vulnerability under the stated reporting standard.

rejected_candidates.title: No minimum amount validation on Compound withdrawals
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as a standalone item. The reportable issue is the specific redeem path that ignores Compound withdrawal failure and still burns full shares; the generic 'no minimum' framing is too broad.

rejected_candidates.title: No validation of external protocol return values
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as a standalone item. The claim is overbroad; most of the cited calls are not independently exploitable, while the materially harmful unchecked-return cases are captured in the specific findings above.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked return value from SafeERC20.approve
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. `safeApprove` does not return an unchecked boolean here; it reverts on low-level failure or on a decoded `false` return.

rejected_candidates.title: No two-step admin transfer or timelock on critical functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. Lack of timelock is governance hardening, not a concrete protocol vulnerability in this threat model; admin transfer itself already uses a pending-admin acceptance flow.

rejected_candidates.title: Division precision loss in interest calculations
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. Integer truncation is expected in fixed-point accounting and no concrete exploit path or material protocol harm was demonstrated.

rejected_candidates.title: Redemption can return less than requested without user notification
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as a standalone item because the material issue is stronger and already captured: the contract can burn the full share amount while paying only the reduced cash amount.

rejected_candidates.title: Exchange rate calculation excludes reserves in certain conditions
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. The cited implementation already subtracts reserves via `getCashExReserves()` before computing `(cash + borrows) / totalSupply`; the candidate does not show a real edge-case bug.
